fact: April 25 is exactly 115 days into the year - that's almost one-third of the whole year gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby rabbits to be born - mama bunnies love having their babies when the weather gets warmer!
Cherry blossom trees in Japan are typically dropping their pink petals like confetti right around this date, creating magical pink carpets on the ground.
In most places north of the equator, you get about 13 hours and 30 minutes of daylight on April 25.
Dandelions are usually at their peak right now in northern climates - those yellow weeds are working overtime to spread their fluffy seeds everywhere!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, April 25 marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red.
The sun typically rises around 6 AM and sets around 7:30 PM in many northern cities, making the days feel perfectly balanced.
Taurus season is in full swing! Anyone born on April 25 is a stubborn bull who probably loves food and cozy blankets.
Migrating birds are flying overhead like crazy right now in North America - millions of warblers, swallows, and other birds are heading north to their summer homes.
In cold mountain areas, this is often when the last stubborn patches of snow finally give up and melt into rushing streams.
Earthworms are having a party in the soil! The ground is finally warm and moist enough for them to wiggle around and make dirt super healthy.
April 25 sits perfectly between the spring equinox and summer solstice - it's like the sweet spot of spring!
